[Maurício Rua](/wiki/Maur%C3%ADcio_Rua "Maurício Rua") [Wanderlei Silva](/wiki/Wanderlei_Silva "Wanderlei Silva") [Cris Cyborg](/wiki/Cris_Cyborg "Cris Cyborg") [Murilo Rua](/wiki/Murilo_Rua "Murilo Rua") [Kazushi Sakuraba](/wiki/Kazushi_Sakuraba "Kazushi Sakuraba") [Daniel Acácio](/wiki/Daniel_Ac%C3%A1cio "Daniel Acácio") Felipe Silva UFC Elizeu Capoeira UFC Matheus Schefel PFL  [Mariusz Linke](/wiki/Mariusz_Linke "Mariusz Linke") [Evangelista Cyborg](/wiki/Evangelista_Santos "Evangelista Santos") [Assuério Silva](/wiki/Assu%C3%A9rio_Silva "Assuério Silva") [Kevin Souza](/wiki/Kevin_Souza "Kevin Souza") Marlon Mathias ([EliteXC](/wiki/EliteXC "EliteXC")) Hélio Dipp ([Jungle Fight](/wiki/Jungle_Fight "Jungle Fight")) Jean Silva ([PRIDE](/wiki/Pride_Fighting_Championships "Pride Fighting Championships")) [Andre Amade](/wiki/Andre_Amade "Andre Amade") José Landi-Jons ([Titan Fighting Championships](/wiki/Titan_Fighting_Championships "Titan Fighting Championships")) Marcos Antonio Santana ([Shooto](/wiki/Shooto "Shooto"))

(born December 3, 1977) is a retired Brazilian [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") and former [Chute Boxe](/wiki/Chute_Boxe "Chute Boxe") [BJJ](/wiki/Brazilian_Jiu-Jitsu "Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu") head coach who formerly fought for the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"). Cristiano competed in the [Brazilian National Jiu-Jitsu Championship](/wiki/Brazilian_National_Jiu-Jitsu_Championship "Brazilian National Jiu-Jitsu Championship") in 1998, 1999 and again in 2001. Marcello was also a competitor on *[The Ultimate Fighter: Live](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Live "The Ultimate Fighter: Live")*.

**Cristiano Marcello da Silva** (born December 3, 1977) is a retired Brazilian [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") and former [Chute Boxe](/wiki/Chute_Boxe "Chute Boxe") [BJJ](/wiki/Brazilian_Jiu-Jitsu "Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu") head coach who formerly fought for the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"). Cristiano competed in the [Brazilian National Jiu-Jitsu Championship](/wiki/Brazilian_National_Jiu-Jitsu_Championship "Brazilian National Jiu-Jitsu Championship") in 1998, 1999 and again in 2001. Marcello was also a competitor on *[The Ultimate Fighter: Live](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Live "The Ultimate Fighter: Live")*.

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristiano_Marcello&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Pride Fighting Championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristiano_Marcello&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Pride Fighting Championships")]

Cristiano fought for [PRIDE Fighting Championships](/wiki/PRIDE_Fighting_Championships "PRIDE Fighting Championships") at the [PRIDE Bushido 12](/wiki/PRIDE_Bushido_12 "PRIDE Bushido 12") in August 2006.

Marcello is widely known for a backstage altercation at [PRIDE Shockwave 2005](/wiki/PRIDE_Shockwave_2005 "PRIDE Shockwave 2005") in which [Charles Bennett](/wiki/Charles_Bennett_(fighter) "Charles Bennett (fighter)") insulted the [Chute Boxe Academy](/wiki/Chute_Boxe_Academy "Chute Boxe Academy") and [Wanderlei Silva](/wiki/Wanderlei_Silva "Wanderlei Silva") resulting in a fight between Marcello and Bennett, and Marcello put Bennett to sleep with a triangle choke before the fight was broken up.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In January 2009, Marcello left the Chute Boxe Academy in Curitiba to go full on with his own martial arts academy, CM System, which opened its doors in March 2009. Despite leaving Chute Boxe, Marcello is still considered a BJJ coach, and continues to train Chute Boxe fighters out of his own gym.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

### The Ultimate Fighter

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristiano_Marcello&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The Ultimate Fighter")]

In February 2012, it was revealed that Marcello was selected to be a participant on *[The Ultimate Fighter: Live](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Live "The Ultimate Fighter: Live")*.[[5]](#cite_note-5) In the opening round, Marcello defeated Jared Carlsten by submission to qualify as one of the 16 fighters to live in the TUF house.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Marcello was selected as Team Faber's second pick (fourth overall). In the second fight of the first round, Marcello was selected to fight [Justin Lawrence](/wiki/Justin_Lawrence_(fighter) "Justin Lawrence (fighter)"), Team Cruz's first overall pick. He was knocked out by Lawrence at 3:15 of the second round.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristiano_Marcello&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Marcello fought fellow cast member [Sam Sicilia](/wiki/Sam_Sicilia "Sam Sicilia") on June 1, 2012 at [The Ultimate Fighter 15 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Live#The_Ultimate_Fighter_15_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Live").[[8]](#cite_note-8) Marcello lost via second round KO due to a flurry of knees and punches.

Cristiano made his return against [Reza Madadi](/wiki/Reza_Madadi "Reza Madadi") on October 13, 2012 at [UFC 153](/wiki/UFC_153 "UFC 153").[[9]](#cite_note-9) He won the fight via a controversial split decision. The decision was controversial since most major MMA outlets scored the bout for Madadi.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Marcello took on UFC newcomer and [World Victory Road](/wiki/World_Victory_Road "World Victory Road") veteran [Kazuki Tokudome](/wiki/Kazuki_Tokudome "Kazuki Tokudome") at [UFC on Fuel TV 8](/wiki/UFC_on_Fuel_TV_8 "UFC on Fuel TV 8") on March 2, 2013.[[14]](#cite_note-14) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.

Marcello faced [Joe Proctor](/wiki/Joe_Proctor "Joe Proctor") on February 15, 2014 at [UFC Fight Night 36](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_36 "UFC Fight Night 36").[[15]](#cite_note-15) He lost the fight via unanimous decision and announced his retirement 2 months later to focus more on his gym and coaching, Marcello also plans to compete in the [ADCC](/wiki/ADCC_Submission_Wrestling_World_Championship "ADCC Submission Wrestling World Championship") for the first time in his grappling career.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

### Return from retirement

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristiano_Marcello&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Return from retirement")]

On June 29, 2018 Marcello announced that he has returned to MMA and has signed a three-fight contract with [Brave CF](/wiki/Brave_Combat_Federation "Brave Combat Federation").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

## Championships and accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristiano_Marcello&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Championships and accomplishments")]

* **Nitrix Champion Fight**
  + Nitrix Lightweight Championship (One time)
